title: Civil engineering market study
text:
Administrative timetable



Date
Action





18 June 2026
Statutory deadline



May 2026
Expected publication of final report



28 January 2026
Deadline for responses to interim report consultation



17 December 2025
Publication of interim report



19 June 2025
Launch of market study




Read our guide to find out more about what to expect at important stages of the market study and how to engage with us. We will update this throughout the study.

How to engage with our civil engineering market study

Update on final report

2 March 2026: The CMA intends to publish the final report in May 2026 after the elections taking place across the UK on Thursday 7 May 2026 and the associated pre-election restrictions for civil servants conclude. We will publish the final report on this case page.

Responses to interim report

2 March 2026: The CMA has published responses to its interim report.

Qualitative research

2 March 2026: The CMA has published a qualitative research report from Jigsaw Research.


Qualitative research with civil engineers (Jigsaw Research) (PDF, 1.1MB) (2.3.26)

Interim report

17 December 2025: The CMA has published an interim report on its civil engineering market study setting out its emerging findings and possible remedies. We invited responses by 28 January 2026.


Executive summary (PDF, 304KB) (17.12.25)


Interim report (PDF, 912KB) (17.12.25)


Appendix A: Further evidence (PDF, 343KB) (17.12.25)


Glossary (PDF, 111KB) (17.12.25)


Qualitative research debrief (PDF, 1.4MB) (17.12.25)

Press release: Civil engineering: CMA sets out concerns and options for better outcomes (17.12.25)

Responses to invitation to comment (at study launch)

26 August 2025: The CMA has published responses to its invitation to comment on its civil engineering market study.


Association for Consultancy and Engineering (ACE) Group (PDF, 345KB) (26.8.25)


Amey Limited (PDF, 142KB) (26.8.25)

Launch of market study

19 June 2025: The CMA launched a market study into the design, planning and delivery of railway and public road infrastructure by the civil engineering sector in the UK.

We invited views by 17 July 2025 from all interested parties on the areas covered in the invitation to comment.


Market study notice (PDF, 101KB) (19.6.25)




Statement of scope (PDF, 372KB) (19.6.25)




Press release: CMA launches review of civil engineering for roads and railways (19.6.25)



Contact

civilengineering@cma.gov.uk

Personal information

The CMA may collect, use and share personal data for its market studies under the Enterprise Act 2002. This includes processing personal data for the purposes of the UK General Data Protection Regulation and the Data Protection Act 2018.

For more information about how the CMA handles personal data, visit the CMA’s Personal information charter.
description: The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is undertaking a market study into the supply of railway and public road infrastructure by the civil engineering sector.
